:= map[string]interface{}{\n239\t\t\t\t\"name\": \"test\",\n240\t\t\t\t\"port\": 8080,\n241\t\t\t}\n242\t\t\treturn Ok[map[string]interface{}, *EnhancedParseError](config)\n243\t\t}\n244\t\n245\t\t// Test cases\n246\t\tcases := []string{\"config.yaml\", \"nonexistent.yaml\", \"invalid.yaml\"}\n247\t\n248\t\tfor _, path := range cases {\n249\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"\\nParsing %s:\\n\", path)\n250\t\t\tresult := parseConfig(path)\n251\t\n252\t\t\tif result.IsOk() {\n253\t\t\t\tconfig := result.Unwrap()\n254\t\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"  Success: %+v\\n\", config)\n255\t\t\t} else {\n256\t\t\t\terr := result.UnwrapErr()\n257\t\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", err.Error())\n258\t\t\t}\n259\t\t}\n260\t\n261\t\t// Output:\n262\t\t// Parsing config.yaml:\n263\t\t//   Success: map[name:test port:8080]\n264\t\t//\n265\t\t// Parsing nonexistent.yaml:\n266\t\t//   Error: io error in nonexistent.yaml: file not found\n267\t\t//\n268\t\t// Parsing invalid.yaml:\n269\t\t// backward compatibility with legacy error types.\n413\tfunc ExampleEnhancedParseError_toLegacy() {\n414\t\tsyntaxErr := NewSyntaxParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"syntax error\", 5, 10, \"key\", \"value\")\n415\t\tlegacySyntax := syntaxErr.ToLegacySyntaxError()\n416\t\tif legacySyntax != nil {\n417\t\t\tfmt.Println(\"Legacy SyntaxError:\", {\n429\t\t\tfmt.Println(\"Legacy TypeMismatchError:\", legacyType.Error())\n430\t\t}\n431\t\n432\t\t// Output:\n433\t\t// Legacy SyntaxError: syntax error in config.yaml at line 5, column 10: syntax error\n434\t\t// Legacy StructureError: structure error in data.yaml at line 10: duplicate key\n435\t\t// Legacy TypeMismatchError: type mismatch in config.yaml at line 8, field field: expected int, got string\n436\t}\n437\t\n438\t// Example 15: Comprehensive Error Handling Pattern\n439\t//\n440\t// This example demonstrates a comprehensive error handling pattern using\n441\t// Result[T, *EnhancedParseError] with type-specific error handling.\n442\tfunc ExampleResult_comprehensiveErrorHandling() {\n443\t\tparseAndValidate := func(path string) Result[map[string]interface{}, *EnhancedParseError] {\n444\t\t\t// Simulate parsing with different error scenarios\n445\t\t\tswitch {\n446\t\t\tcase strings.Contains(path, \"missing\"):\n447\t\t\t\treturn Err[map[string]interface{}, *EnhancedParseError](\n448\t\t\t\t\tNewValidationParseError(path, \"required field missing\", 5, \"database.host\", \"required\", \"field is required\"),\n449\t\t\t\t)\n450\t\t\tcase strings.Contains(path, \"invalid\"):\n451\t\t\t\treturn Err[map[string]interface{}, *EnhancedParseError](\n452\t\t\t\t\tNewSyntaxParseError(path, \"invalid YAML syntax\", 10, 5, \"valid YAML\", \"invalid\"),\n453\t\t\t\t)\n454\t\t\tcase strings.Contains(path, \"type\"):\n455\t\t\t\treturn Err[map[string]interface{}, *EnhancedParseError](\n456\t\t\t\t\tNewTypeMismatchParseError(path, \"type mismatch\", 8, \"server.port\", \"integer\", \"string\", \"\\\"8080\\\"\"),\n457\t\t\t\t)\n458\t\t\tdefault:\n459\t\t\t\tconfig := map[string]interface{}{\n460\t\t\t\t\t\"database\": map[string]interface{}{\n461\t\t\t\t\t\t\"host\": \"localhost\",\n462\t\t\t\t\t\t\"port\": 5432,\n463\t\t\t\t\t},\n464\t\t\t\t}\n465\t\t\t\treturn Ok[map[string]interface{}, *EnhancedParseError](config)\n466\t\t\t}\n467\t\t}\n468\t\n469\t\t// Test different scenarios\n470\t\tscenarios := []string{\"config.yaml\", \"missing_field.yaml\", \"invalid_syntax.yaml\", \"type_error.yaml\"}\n471\t\n472\t\tfor _, path := range scenarios {\n473\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"\\n=== Testing %s ===\\n\", path)\n474\t\t\tresult := parseAndValidate(path)\n475\t\n476\t\t\tif result.IsOk() {\n477\t\t\t\tconfig := result.Unwrap()\n478\t\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"✓ Success: Config loaded with %d keys\\n\", len(config))\n479\t\t\t} else {\n480\t\t\t\terr := result.UnwrapErr()\n481\t\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"✗ Error: %s\\n\", err.Error())\n482\t\n483\t\t\t\t// Type-specific handling\n484\t\t\t\tswitch {\n485\t\t\t\tcase err.IsSyntaxError():\n486\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Println(\"  → Check YAML syntax and indentation\")\n487\t\t\t\tcase err.IsStructureError():\n488\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Println(\"  → Check YAML structure for duplicate keys or invalid nesting\")\n489\t\t\t\tcase err.IsTypeMismatchError():\n490\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Println(\"  → Verify field types match expected types\")\n491\t\t\t\tcase err.IsValidationError():\n492\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Println(\"  → Ensure all required fields are present and constraints are met\")\n493\t\t\t\tcase err.IsSchemaError():\n494\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Println(\"  → Validate schema definition and file path\")\n495\t\t\t\tcase err.IsIOError():\n496\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Println(\"  → Check file permissions and path\")\n497\t\t\t\tcase err.IsEmpty():\n498\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Println(\"  → File is empty, add YAML content\")\n499\t\t\t\tdefault:\n500\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Println(\"  → Review error details\")\n501\t\t\t\t}\n502\t\t\t}\n503\t\t}\n504\t\n505\t\t// Output:\n506\t\t// === Testing config.yaml ===\n507\t\t// ✓ Success: Config loaded with 1 keys\n508\t\t//\n509\t\t// === Testing missing_field.yaml ===\n510\t\t// ✗ Error: validation error in missing_field.yaml at line 5: required field missing (field: database.host, constraint: field is required)\n511\t\t//   → Ensure all required fields are present and constraints are met\n512\t\t//\n513\t\t// === Testing invalid_syntax.yaml ===\n514\t\t// ✗ Error: syntax error in invalid_syntax.yaml at line 10, column 5: invalid YAML syntax (expected: valid YAML, found: invalid)\n515\t\t//   → Check YAML syntax and indentation\n516\t\t//\n517\t\t// === Package yamlutil provides comprehensive examples demonstrating the ParseError design.\n//\n// This file contains examples showing how to use the EnhancedParseError type\n// with the Result[T, E] failed\",\n\t\t20,\n\t\t\"/path/to/schema.yaml\",\n\t\t\"DatabaseConfig\",\n\t)\n\n\tfmt.Println(err.Error())\n\tfmt.Println(\"Schema path:\", err.Detail.SchemaPath)\n\tfmt.Println(\"Schema name:\", Action: Check YAML syntax and indentation\n}\n\n// Example 14: Converting to Legacy Error Types\n//\n// This example demonstrates backward compatibility with legacy error types.\nfunc ExampleEnhancedParseError_toLegacy() {\n\tsyntaxErr := NewSyntaxParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"syntax error\", 5, 10, \"key\", \"value\")\n\tlegacySyntax := syntaxErr.ToLegacySyntaxError()\n\tif {\n266\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n267\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")  // Add 2-space prefix for consistency\n268\t\t\tsb.WriteString(e.LocationInfo.Snippet)\n269\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n270\t\n271\t\t\t// Add column indicator if available\n272\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 && e.LocationInfo.Column <= len(e.LocationInfo.Snippet) {\n273\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")  // Add 2-space prefix for consistency\n274\t\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < e.LocationInfo.Column-1; i++ {\n275\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" \")\n276\t\t\t\t}\n277\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"^--- here\\n\")\n278\t\t\t}\n279\t\t}\n280\t\n281\t\t// Add surrounding context if available\n282\t\tif len(e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines) > 0 {\n283\t\t\t// Add blank line separator if we showed the snippet\n284\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n285\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n286\t\t\t}\n287\t\t\tfor i, line := range e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines {\n288\t\t\t\tif i == e.LocationInfo.SnippetLineIndex {\n289\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"> \")\n290\t\t\t\t} else {\n291\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n292\t\t\t\t}\n293\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(line)\n294\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n295\t\t\t}\n296\t\t}\n297\t\n298\t\treturn sb.String()\n299\t}\n300\t\n301\t// IsSyntaxError checks if this is a syntax error.\n302\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSyntaxError() bool {\n303\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSyntax\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t// IsStructureError checks if this is a structure error.\n307\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsStructureError() bool {\n308\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindStructure\n309\t}\n310\t\n311\t// IsTypeMismatchError checks if this is a type mismatch error.\n312\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsTypeMismatchError() bool {\n313\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch\n314\t}\n315\t\n316\t// IsIOError checks if this is an I/O error.\n317\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsIOError() bool {\n318\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindIO\n319\t}\n320\t\n321\t// IsValidationError checks if this is a validation error.\n322\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsValidationError() bool {\n323\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindValidation\n324\t}\n325\t\n326\t// IsSchemaError checks if this is a schema error.\n327\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSchemaError() bool {\n328\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSchema\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// IsEmpty checks if this is an empty file error.\n332\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsEmpty() bool {\n333\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindEmpty\n334\t}\n335\t\n336\t// ============================================================================\n337\t// Error Construction Helpers\n338\t// ============================================================================\n339\t\n340\t// NewSyntaxParseError creates a new syntax error.\n341\tfunc NewSyntaxParseError(filePath, message string, line, column int, expected, found string) *EnhancedParseError {\n342\t\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n343\t\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindSyntax,\n344\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n345\t\t\tMessage:  message,\n346\t\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n347\t\t\t\tLine:   line,\n348\t\t\t\tColumn: column,\n349\t\t\t},\n350\t\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n351\t\t\t\tExpected: expected,\n352\t\t\t\tFound:    found,\n353\t\t\t},\n354\t\t}\n355\t}\n356\t\n357\t// NewStructureParseError creates a new structure error.\n358\tfunc NewStructureParseError(filePath, message string, line int, duplicateKey, location string) *EnhancedParseError {\n359\t\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n360\t\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindStructure,\n361\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n362\t\t\tMessage:  message,\n363\t\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n364\t\t\t\tLine: line,\n365\t\t\t},\n366\t\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n367\t\t\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n368\t\t\t\tLocation:     location,\n369\t\t\t},\n370\t\t}\n371\t}\n372\t\n373\t// NewTypeMismatchParseError creates a new type mismatch error.\n374\tfunc NewTypeMismatchParseError(filePath, message string, line int, fieldPath, expectedType, actualType, value string) *EnhancedParseError {\n375\t\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n376\t\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch,\n377\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n378\t\t\tMessage:  message,\n379\t\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n380\t\t\t\tLine: line,\n381\t\t\t},\n382\t\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n383\t\t\t\tFieldPath:    fieldPath,\n384\t\t\t\tExpectedType: expectedType,\n385\t\t\t\tActualType:   actualType,\n386\t\t\t\tValue:        value,\n387\t\t\t},\n388\t\t}\n389\t}\n390\t\n391\t// NewIOParseError creates a new I/O error.\n392\tfunc NewIOParseError(filePath, message string, line int, underlyingErr error) *EnhancedParseError {\n393\t\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n394\t\t\tKind:          ParseErrorKindIO,\n395\t\t\tFilePath:      filePath,\n396\t\t\tMessage:       message,\n397\t\t\tUnderlyingErr: underlyingErr,\n398\t\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n399\t\t\t\tLine: ============================================================================\n447\t// Error Propagation Strategy\n448\t// ============================================================================\n449\t\n450\t// The error propagation strategy for ParseError follows these principles:\n451\t//\n452\t// 1. Always provide rich context: Include line numbers, column positions, and\n453\t//    source snippets whenever possible.\n454\t//\n455\t// 2. Preserve error chain: Use error wrapping (errors.Wrap, fmt.Errorf with %w)\n456\t//    to maintain the full error chain for debugging.\n457\t//\n458\t// 3. Type-safe error handling: Use Result[T, ParseError] pattern for compile-time\n459\t//    error handling guarantees.\n460\t//\n461\t// 4. Error transformation: Transform lower-level errors (e.g., yaml.v3 errors)\n462\t//    into EnhancedParseError with appropriate context and snippets.\n463\t//\n464\t// 5. Fail-fast on critical errors: I/O errors and syntax errors should immediately\n465\t//    return errors without attempting further processing.\n466\t//\n467\t// Error Flow:\n468\t//   Raw Error → EnhancedParseError (with context) → Result[T, ParseError]\n469\t//\n470\t// Example propagation:\n471\t//   yaml.Unmarshal error → extract line/column → read source snippet →\n472\t//   NewSyntaxParseError with snippet → Result[T, ParseError]\n473\t\n474\t// ============================================================================\n475\t// Integration with Legacy Error Types\n476\t// ============================================================================\n477\t\n478\t// ToLegacySyntaxError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy SyntaxError.\n479\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacySyntaxError() *SyntaxError {\n480\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindSyntax {\n481\t\t\treturn nil\n482\t\t}\n483\t\treturn &SyntaxError{\n484\t\t\tFilePath:  e.FilePath,\n485\t\t\tLine:      e.LocationInfo.Line,\n486\t\t\tColumn:    e.LocationInfo.Column,\n487\t\t\tMessage:   e.Message,\n488\t\t\tExpected:  e.Detail.Expected,\n489\t\t\tFound:     e.Detail.Found,\n490\t\t\tErr:       e.UnderlyingErr,\n491\t\t\tErrorCode: e.Code(),\n492\t\t}\n493\t}\n494\t\n495\t// ToLegacyStructureError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy StructureError.\n496\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyStructureError() *StructureError {\n497\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindStructure {\n498\t\t\treturn nil\n499\t\t}\n500\t\treturn &StructureError{\n501\t\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n502\t\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n503\t\t\tMessage:      e.Message,\n504\t\t\tDuplicateKey: e.Detail.DuplicateKey,\n505\t\t\tLocation:     e.Detail.Location,\n506\t\t\tErr:          e.UnderlyingErr,\n507\t\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n508\t\t}\n509\t}\n510\t\n511\t// ToLegacyTypeMismatchError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy TypeMismatchError.\n512\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyTypeMismatchError() *TypeMismatchError {\n513\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch {\n514\t\t\treturn nil\n515\t\t}\n516\t\treturn string) (string, error)\n153\t\n154\t\t// GetRequiredInt retrieves an integer field value, returning an error if missing or not numeric.\n155\t\t// Returns the integer value or FieldNotFoundError if missing, TypeMismatchError if not numeric.\n156\t\tGetRequiredInt(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (int, error)\n157\t\n158\t\t// GetRequiredBool retrieves a boolean field value, returning an error if missing or not boolean.\n159\t\t// Returns the boolean value or FieldNotFoundError if missing, TypeMismatchError if not boolean.\n160\t\tGetRequiredBool(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (bool, error)\n161\t\n162\t\t// ValidateRequiredFields checks that all required field paths exist in the YAML data.\n163\t\t// Returns a list of missing field paths. Empty list means all required fields are present.\n164\t\tValidateRequiredFields(data map[string]interface{}, requiredFields []string) []string\n165\t\n166\t\t// ValidateFieldRequirements validates field requirements with type checking.\n167\t\t// Returns a list of validation errors. Empty list means all requirements passed.\n168\t\tValidateFieldRequirements(data map[string]interface{}, requirements []FieldRequirement) []error\n169\t}\n170\t\n171\t// YAMLErrorHandler defines the interface for handling YAML processing errors.\n172\t//\n173\t// ErrorHandlers can implement different error handling strategies,\n174\t// such as logging, recovery, or custom error formatting.\n175\ttype YAMLErrorHandler interface {\n176\t\t// HandleFileError handles file I/O errors during YAML processing.\n177\t\tHandleFileError(err error, filePath string)\n178\t\n179\t\t// HandleParseError handles YAML parsing errors.\n180\t\tHandleParseError(err error, filePath string)\n181\t\n182\t\t// HandleValidationError handles YAML validation errors.\n183\t\tHandleValidationError(errs []ValidationError, filePath string)\n184\t}\n185\t\n186\t// YAMLReader defines the interface for reading YAML from various sources.\n187\t//\n188\t// This interface abstracts the source of YAML content, enabling\n189\t// reading from files, streams, memory, or network sources.\n190\ttype YAMLReader interface {\n191\t\t// ReadYAML reads YAML content from the source.\n192\t\t// Returns the content as a byte slice and any error encountered.\n193\t\tReadYAML() ([]byte, error)\n194\t}\n195\t\n196\t// FileDiscovery defines the interface for discovering YAML files in filesystems.\n197\t//\n198\t// FileDiscovery implementations can find YAML files using different\n199\t// strategies and filtering criteria.\n200\ttype FileDiscovery interface {\n201\t\t// FindYAMLFiles finds all YAML files in a directory (non-recursive).\n202\t\tFindYAMLFiles(dirPath string) ([]string, error)\n203\t\n204\t\t// FindYAMLFilesRecursive finds all YAML files in a directory recursively.\n205\t\tFindYAMLFilesRecursive(dirPath string) ([]string, error)\n206\t\n207\t\t// IsYAMLFile checks if a file has a YAML extension (.yaml or .yml).\n208\t\tIsYAMLFile(filePath string) bool\n209\t}\n210\t\n211\t// StreamYAMLParser defines the interface for streaming YAML parsing.\n212\t//\n213\t// This interface is reserved for future implementation of streaming\n214\t// support for large YAML files.\n215\ttype StreamYAMLParser interface {\n216\t\t// ParseStream parses YAML content from a reader stream.\n217\t\tParseStream(reader io.Reader, data interface{}) error\n218\t\n219\t\t// ParseStreamToMap parses YAML content from a reader into a generic map.\n220\t\tParseStreamToMap(reader io.Reader) (map[string]interface{}, error)\n221\t}\n222\t\n223\t// YAMLConverter defines the interface for converting YAML data structures.\n224\t//\n225\t// Converters can transform YAML data between different formats\n226\t// or apply transformations to the data structure.\n227\ttype YAMLConverter interface {\n228\t\t// ToJSON converts YAML data to JSON format.\n229\t\tToJSON(yamlData map[string]interface{}) ([]byte, error)\n230\t\n231\t\t// ToXML converts YAML data to XML format.\n232\t\tToXML(yamlData map[string]interface{}) ([]byte, error)\n233\t\n234\t\t// ToEnv converts YAML data to environment variable format.\n235\t\tToEnv(yamlData map[string]interface{}, prefix string) ([]string, error)\n236\t\n237\t\t// Merge merges multiple YAML data structures into one.\n238\t\tMerge(datas ...map[string]interface{}) (map[string]interface{}, error)\n239\t}\n240\t\n241\t// YAMLPathNavigator defines the interface for advanced path navigation.\n242\t//\n243\t// PathNavigators can support more complex path expressions beyond\n244\t// simple dot notation, including array indexing and wildcards.\n245\ttype YAMLPathNavigator interface {\n246\t\t// GetPath retrieves a value using a complex path expression.\n247\t\t// Supports array indexing, wildcards, and other advanced patterns.\n248\t\tGetPath(data map[string]interface{}, pathExpr string) (interface{}, error)\n249\t\n250\t\t// SetPath sets a value at a complex path expression.\n251\t\tSetPath(data map[string]interface{}, pathExpr string, value interface{}) error\n252\t\n253\t\t// DeletePath removes a value at a complex path expression.\n254\t\tDeletePath(data map[string]interface{}, pathExpr string) error\n255\t}\n256\t\n257\t// YAMLCache defines the interface for caching parsed YAML content.\n258\t//\n259\t// Caches can improve performance by avoiding repeated parsing\n260\t// of frequently accessed configuration files.\n261\ttype YAMLCache interface {\n262\t\t// Get retrieves cached parsed YAML data if available.\n263\t\tGet(filePath string) (map[string]interface{}, bool)\n264\t\n265\t\t// Set stores parsed YAML data in the cache.\n266\t\tSet(filePath string, data map[string]interface{})\n267\t\n268\t\t// Invalidate removes a file from the cache.\n269\t\tInvalidate(filePath string)\n270\t\n271\t\t// Clear removes all entries from the cache.\n272\t\tClear()\n273\t\n274\t\t// Size returns the number of entries in the cache.\n275\t\tSize() int\n276\t}\n277\t\n278\t// YAMLWatcher defines the interface for watching YAML files for changes.\n279\t//\n280\t// Watchers can monitor files for modifications and trigger callbacks,\n281\t// enabling hot-reload configuration capabilities.\n282\ttype YAMLWatcher interface {\n283\t\t// Watch starts monitoring a file for changes.\n284\t\t// Returns a channel that receives events when the file changes.\n285\t\tWatch(filePath string) (<-chan FileChangeEvent, error)\n286\t\n287\t\t// Unwatch stops monitoring a file.\n288\t\tUnwatch(filePath string) error\n289\t\n290\t\t// Close stops all watching and releases resources.\n291\t\tClose() error\n292\t}\n293\t\n294\t// FileChangeEvent represents a change to a monitored file.\n295\ttype FileChangeEvent struct {\n296\t\tFilePath  string\n297\t\tEventType FileEventType\n298\t}\n299\t\n300\t// FileEventType represents the type of file change event.\n301\ttype FileEventType int\n302\t\n303\tconst (\n304\t\tFileCreated FileEventType = iota\n305\t\tFileModified\n306\t\tFileDeleted\n307\t)\n308\t\n309\t// YAMLProcessor defines a comprehensive interface combining multiple YAML operations.\n310\t//\n311\t// This interface provides a complete YAML processing facility that can parse,\n312\t// validate, access fields, and handle errors in a unified way.\n313\ttype YAMLProcessor interface {\n314\t\tFileReader\n315\t\tYAMLParser\n316\t\tYAMLValidator\n317\t\tFieldAccessor\n318\t\tFileDiscovery\n319\t}\n320\t\n321\t// {\n375\t\treturn ReadFile(path)\n376\t}\n377\t\n378\t// Exists implements FileReader.Exists by delegating to the FileExists function.\n379\tfunc (d *defaultFileReader) Exists(path string) bool path)\n406\t}\n407\t\n408\t// GetRequiredField implements FieldAccessor.GetRequiredField by delegating to the GetRequiredField function.\n409\tfunc (d *defaultFieldAccessor) GetRequiredField(data path)\n416\t}\n417\t\n418\t// GetRequiredInt implements FieldAccessor.GetRequiredInt by delegating to the GetRequiredInt function.\n419\tfunc (d *defaultFieldAccessor) GetRequiredInt(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (int, error) {\n420\t\treturn GetRequiredInt(data, path)\n421\t}\n422\t\n423\t// GetRequiredBool implements FieldAccessor.GetRequiredBool by delegating to the GetRequiredBool function.\n424\tfunc (d *defaultFieldAccessor) GetRequiredBool(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (bool, error) {\n425\t\treturn GetRequiredBool(data, path)\n426\t}\n427\t\n428\t// ValidateRequiredFields implements FieldAccessor.ValidateRequiredFields by delegating to the ValidateRequiredFields function.\n429\tfunc (d *defaultFieldAccessor) ValidateRequiredFields(data map[string]interface{}, requiredFields []string) []string {\n430\t\treturn ValidateRequiredFields(data, requiredFields)\n431\t}\n432\t\n433\t// ValidateFieldRequirements implements FieldAccessor.ValidateFieldRequirements by delegating to the ValidateFieldRequirements function.\n434\tfunc (d *defaultFieldAccessor) ValidateFieldRequirements(data map[string]interface{}, requirements []FieldRequirement) []error {\n435\t\treturn ValidateFieldRequirements(data, requirements)\n436\t}\n437\t\n438\t// FindYAMLFiles implements FileDiscovery.FindYAMLFiles by delegating to the FindYAMLFiles function.\n439\tfunc (d *defaultFileDiscovery) FindYAMLFiles(dirPath string) ([]string, error) {\n440\t\treturn FindYAMLFiles(dirPath)\n441\t}\n442\t\n443\t// FindYAMLFilesRecursive implements FileDiscovery.FindYAMLFilesRecursive by delegating to the FindYAMLFilesRecursive function.\n444\tfunc (d *defaultFileDiscovery) FindYAMLFilesRecursive(dirPath string) ([]string, error) {\n445\t\treturn FindYAMLFilesRecursive(dirPath)\n446\t}\n447\t\n448\t// IsYAMLFile implements FileDiscovery.IsYAMLFile by delegating to the IsYAMLFile function.\n449\tfunc (d *defaultFileDiscovery) IsYAMLFile(filePath string) bool {\n450\t\treturn IsYAMLFile(filePath)\n451\t}\n452\t\n453\t// ============================================================================\n454\t// Parser Strategy Implementations\n455\t// ============================================================================\n456\t\n457\t// StandardParser implements YAMLParser using standard parsing with full file loading.\n458\t//\n459\t// StandardParser is the default parsing strategy that loads entire files into memory\n460\t// before parsing. This is suitable for most configuration files and provides good\n461\t// performance for small to medium-sized YAML files.\n462\ttype StandardParser struct {\n463\t\tparser *Parser\n464\t\tconfig *ParserConfig\n465\t}\n466\t\n467\t// NewStandardParser creates a new StandardParser with default configuration.\n468\t//\n469\t// Returns a StandardParser ready to parse YAML files with sensible defaults.\n470\tfunc NewStandardParser() *StandardParser {\n471\t\treturn &StandardParser{\n472\t\t\tparser: NewParser(),\n473\t\t\tconfig: DefaultParserConfig(),\n474\t\t}\n475\t}\n476\t\n477\t// NewStandardParserWithConfig creates a new StandardParser with custom configuration.\n478\t//\n479\t// Allows fine-grained control over parser behavior including strict mode,\n480\t// error handling, caching, and performance tuning.\n481\tfunc NewStandardParserWithConfig(config *ParserConfig) *StandardParser {\n482\t\treturn &StandardParser{\n483\t\t\tparser: NewParser(),\n484\t\t\tconfig: config,\n485\t\t}\n486\t}\n487\t\n488\t// ParseFile implements YAMLParser.ParseFile by delegating to the underlying Parser.\n489\tfunc (sp *StandardParser) ParseFile(filePath string, data interface{}) ParseResult {\n490\t\treturn sp.parser.ParseFile(filePath, data)\n491\t}\n492\t\n493\t// ParseFileToMap implements YAMLParser.ParseFileToMap by delegating to the underlying Parser.\n494\tfunc (sp *StandardParser) ParseFileToMap(filePath string) ParseResult {\n495\t\treturn sp.parser.ParseFileToMap(filePath)\n496\t}\n497\t\n498\t// ParseString implements YAMLParser.ParseString by delegating to the underlying Parser.\n499\tfunc (sp *StandardParser) ParseString(yamlContent string, data interface{}) error {\n500\t\treturn sp.parser.ParseString(yamlContent, data)\n501\t}\n502\t\n503\t// MustParseFile implements YAMLParser.MustParseFile by delegating to the underlying Parser.\n504\tfunc (sp *StandardParser) MustParseFile(filePath string, data interface{}) {\n505\t\tsp.parser.MustParseFile(filePath, data)\n506\t}\n507\t\n508\t// Config implements YAMLParser.Config by returning the parser configuration.\n509\tfunc (sp *StandardParser) Config() *ParserConfig {\n510\t\treturn sp.config\n511\t}\n512\t\n513\t// CachedParser implements YAMLParser with caching support for repeated access.\n514\t//\n515\t// CachedParser wraps another parser and caches parsed results to avoid re-parsing\n516\t// the same file multiple times. This is useful for frequently accessed configuration\n517\t// files or when parsing is expensive.\n518\t//\n519\t// The cache respects the configuration's CacheTTL and MaxCacheSize settings.\n520\t// Entries are evicted based on LRU policy when the cache is full.\n521\ttype CachedParser struct {\n522\t\tparser     YAMLParser\n523\t\tcache      map[string]*cachedEntry\n524\t\tconfig     *ParserConfig\n525\t\tcacheStats CacheStats\n526\t}\n527\t\n528\t// CacheStats contains statistics about parser cache usage.\n529\t//\n530\t// CacheStats provides metrics for cache performance monitoring and tuning.\n531\ttype CacheStats struct {\n532\t\tHits      int // Number of cache hits\n533\t\tMisses    int // Number of cache misses\n534\t\tSize      int // Current number of cached items\n535\t\tMaxSize   int // Maximum cache size\n536\t\tEvictions int // Number of cache evictions\n537\t}\n538\t\n539\ttype cachedEntry struct {\n540\t\tdata      interface{}\n541\t\tresult    ParseResult\n542\t\ttimestamp int64 // Unix nanoseconds\n543\t\taccesses  int\n544\t}\n545\t\n546\t// NewCachedParser creates a new CachedParser wrapping the given parser.\n547\t//\n548\t// The returned parser will cache results from the wrapped parser according\n549\t// to the default caching configuration.\n550\tfunc NewCachedParser(parser YAMLParser) *CachedParser {\n551\t\treturn &CachedParser{\n552\t\t\tparser: parser,\n553\t\t\tcache:  make(map[string]*cachedEntry),\n554\t\t\tconfig: DefaultParserConfig(),\n555\t\t}\n556\t}\n557\t\n558\t// NewCachedParserWithConfig creates a new CachedParser with custom configuration.\n559\t//\n560\t// Allows control over cache behavior including TTL, maximum size, and\n561\t// whether to cache invalid files.\n562\tfunc NewCachedParserWithConfig(parser YAMLParser, config *ParserConfig) *CachedParser {\n563\t\treturn &CachedParser{\n564\t\t\tparser: parser,\n565\t\t\tcache:  make(map[string]*cachedEntry),\n566\t\t\tconfig: config,\n567\t\t}\n568\t}\n569\t\n570\t// ParseFile implements YAMLParser.ParseFile with caching support.\n571\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) ParseFile(filePath string, data interface{}) ParseResult {\n572\t\t// Check cache if enabled\n573\t\tif cp.config.EnableCaching {\n574\t\t\tif entry, found := cp.getFromCache(filePath); found {\n575\t\t\t\tcp.cacheStats.Hits++\n576\t\t\t\tentry.accesses++\n577\t\t\t\t// Copy cached data to the provided parameter\n578\t\t\t\tif cachedData, ok := entry.data.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n579\t\t\t\t\tif targetMap, ok := data.(*map[string]interface{}); ok {\n580\t\t\t\t\t\t*targetMap = cachedData\n581\t\t\t\t\t}\n582\t\t\t\t}\n583\t\t\t\treturn entry.result\n584\t\t\t}\n585\t\t\tcp.cacheStats.Misses++\n586\t\t}\n587\t\n588\t\t// Parse using the wrapped parser\n589\t\tresult := cp.parser.ParseFile(filePath, data)\n590\t\n591\t\t// Cache the result if enabled and parsing succeeded\n592\t\tif cp.config.EnableCaching && result.Success {\n593\t\t\tcp.addToCache(filePath, data, result)\n594\t\t}\n595\t\n596\t\treturn result\n597\t}\n598\t\n599\t// ParseFileToMap implements YAMLParser.ParseFileToMap with caching support.\n600\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) ParseFileToMap(filePath string) ParseResult {\n601\t\t// Check cache if enabled\n602\t\tif cp.config.EnableCaching {\n603\t\t\tif entry, found := cp.getFromCache(filePath); found {\n604\t\t\t\tcp.cacheStats.Hits++\n605\t\t\t\tentry.accesses++\n606\t\t\t\treturn entry.result\n607\t\t\t}\n608\t\t\tcp.cacheStats.Misses++\n609\t\t}\n610\t\n611\t\t// Parse using the wrapped parser\n612\t\tresult := cp.parser.ParseFileToMap(filePath)\n613\t\n614\t\t// Cache the result if enabled and parsing succeeded\n615\t\tif cp.config.EnableCaching && result.Success {\n616\t\t\tcp.addToCache(filePath, result.Data, result)\n617\t\t}\n618\t\n619\t\treturn result\n620\t}\n621\t\n622\t// ParseString implements YAMLParser.ParseString by delegating to the wrapped parser.\n623\t//\n624\t// Note: ParseString results are not cached as they are typically one-off operations.\n625\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) ParseString(yamlContent string, data interface{}) error {\n626\t\treturn cp.parser.ParseString(yamlContent, data)\n627\t}\n628\t\n629\t// MustParseFile implements YAMLParser.MustParseFile by delegating to the wrapped parser.\n630\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) MustParseFile(filePath string, data interface{}) {\n631\t\tresult := cp.ParseFile(filePath, data)\n632\t\tif !result.Success {\n633\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to parse YAML file %s: %v\", filePath, result.Error))\n634\t\t}\n635\t}\n636\t\n637\t// Config implements YAMLParser.Config by returning the parser configuration.\n638\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) Config() *ParserConfig {\n639\t\treturn cp.config\n640\t}\n641\t\n642\t// ClearCache clears all cached entries.\n643\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) ClearCache() {\n644\t\tcp.cache = make(map[string]*cachedEntry)\n645\t}\n646\t\n647\t// CacheSize returns the current number of cached entries.\n648\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) CacheSize() int {\n649\t\treturn len(cp.cache)\n650\t}\n651\t\n652\t// CacheStats returns statistics about cache usage.\n653\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) CacheStats() CacheStats {\n654\t\tcp.cacheStats.Size = len(cp.cache)\n655\t\treturn cp.cacheStats\n656\t}\n657\t\n658\t// getFromCache retrieves an entry from the cache if it exists and hasn't expired.\n659\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) getFromCache(filePath string) (*cachedEntry, bool) {\n660\t\tentry, found := cp.cache[filePath]\n661\t\tif !found {\n662\t\t\treturn nil, false\n663\t\t}\n664\t\n665\t\t// Check TTL\n666\t\tif cp.config.CacheTTL > 0 {\n667\t\t\tage := time.Now().UnixNano() - entry.timestamp\n668\t\t\tif age > int64(cp.config.CacheTTL) {\n669\t\t\t\tdelete(cp.cache, filePath)\n670\t\t\t\treturn nil, false\n671\t\t\t}\n672\t\t}\n673\t\n674\t\treturn entry, true\n675\t}\n676\t\n677\t// addToCache adds a result to the cache, evicting entries if necessary.\n678\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) addToCache(filePath string, data interface{}, result ParseResult) {\n679\t\t// Check if we need to evict entries\n680\t\tif cp.config.MaxCacheSize > 0 && len(cp.cache) >= cp.config.MaxCacheSize {\n681\t\t\tcp.evictLRU()\n682\t\t}\n683\t\n684\t\tcp.cache[filePath] = &cachedEntry{\n685\t\t\tdata:      data,\n686\t\t\tresult:    result,\n687\t\t\ttimestamp: time.Now().UnixNano(),\n688\t\t\taccesses:  1,\n689\t\t}\n690\t}\n691\t\n692\t// evictLRU evicts the least recently used cache entry.\n693\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) evictLRU() {\n694\t\tvar lruPath string\n695\t\tvar lruAccesses int = int(^uint(0) >> 1) // Max int\n696\t\n697\t\tfor path, entry := range cp.cache {\n698\t\t\tif entry.accesses < lruAccesses {\n699\t\t\t\tlruAccesses = entry.accesses\n700\t\t\t\tlruPath = path\n701\t\t\t}\n702\t\t}\n703\t\n704\t\tif lruPath != \"\" {\n705\t\t\tdelete(cp.cache, lruPath)\n706\t\t\tcp.cacheStats.Evictions++\n707\t\t}\n708\t}\n709\t\n710\t// StreamingParser implements YAMLParser with streaming support for large files.\n711\t//\n712\t// StreamingParser reads and processes YAML files incrementally rather than\n713\t// loading the entire file into memory. This is useful for very large YAML\n714\t// files where memory usage is a concern.\n715\t//\n716\t// Note: This is a placeholder for future implementation. ve.Type\n341\t\t}\n342\t\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n343\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n344\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n345\t\t}\n346\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n347\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n348\t\t}\n349\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n350\t}\n351\t\n352\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n353\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n354\t\treturn ve.ContextStr\n355\t}\n356\t\n357\t// Error implements the error interface.\n358\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n359\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n360\t\n361\t\t// Build base error with location\n362\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n363\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line))\n364\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n365\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n366\t\t\t}\n367\t\t} else {\n368\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", ve.FilePath))\n369\t\t}\n370\t\n371\t\t// Add field path if available\n372\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n373\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at field %s\", ve.FieldPath))\n374\t\t}\n375\t\n376\t\t// Add message\n377\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ve.Message))\n378\t\n379\t\t// Add constraint if available\n380\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n381\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (constraint: %s)\", ve.Constraint))\n382\t\t}\n383\t\n384\t\treturn sb.String()\n385\t}\n386\t\n387\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n388\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n389\t\treturn ve.Err\n390\t}\n391\t\n392\t// String returns a formatted error message with context.\n393\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n394\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n395\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n396\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n397\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n398\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n399\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n400\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n401\t\t\t}\n402\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n403\t\t}\n404\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n405\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n406\t\t}\n407\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n408\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n409\t\t}\n410\t\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n411\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n412\t\t}\n413\t\treturn sb.String()\n414\t}\n415\t\n416\t// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n417\t//\n418\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n419\t// ValidationError instances with message, path, constraint, error code, and location.\n420\t//\n421\t// Parameters:\n422\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being validated\n423\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n424\t//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n425\t//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n426\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n427\t//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n428\t//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n429\t//   - errorType: Category of error (use empty string for default ErrorTypeValidation)\n430\t//\n431\t// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n432\t//\n433\t// Example usage:\n434\t//\n435\t//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue, 10, 5, \"\")\n436\tfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath string, constraint string, code ErrorCode, line int, column int, errorType ErrorType) *ValidationError {\n437\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic validation error\n438\t\terrorCode := code\n439\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n440\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeValidationFailed\n441\t\t}\n442\t\n443\t\t// Use provided error type or default to validation error\n444\t\teType := errorType\n445\t\tif eType == \"\" {\n446\t\t\teType = ErrorTypeValidation\n447\t\t}\n448\t\n449\t\treturn &ValidationError{\n450\t\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n451\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n452\t\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n453\t\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n454\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n455\t\t\tLine:       line,\n456\t\t\tColumn:     column,\n457\t\t\tType:       eType,\n458\t\t}\n459\t}\n460\t\n461\t// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\n462\tfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n463\t\tvar ve *ValidationError\n464\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &ve) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n465\t}\n466\t\n467\t// NewFileError creates a new FileError with proper initialization.\n468\tfunc NewFileError(path string, operation string, message string, errorCode ErrorCode) *FileError {\n469\t\treturn &FileError{\n470\t\t\tPath:       path,\n471\t\t\tOperation:  operation,\n472\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n473\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n474\t\t}\n475\t}\n476\t\n477\t// NewSchemaValidationError creates a new SchemaValidationError with proper initialization.\n478\tfunc NewSchemaValidationError(filePath string, schemaPath string, fieldPath string, message string, expected string, found string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *SchemaValidationError {\n479\t\treturn &SchemaValidationError{\n480\t\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n481\t\t\tSchemaPath: schemaPath,\n482\t\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n483\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n484\t\t\tExpected:   expected,\n485\t\t\tFound:      found,\n486\t\t\tLine:       line,\n487\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n488\t\t}\n489\t}\n490\t\n491\t// NewTypeMismatchError creates a new TypeMismatchError with proper initialization.\n492\tfunc NewTypeMismatchError(filePath string, fieldPath string, expectedType string, actualType string, value string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *TypeMismatchError    value,\n499\t\t\tLine:         line,\n500\t\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n501\t\t}\n502\t}\n503\t\n504\t// NewFieldNotFoundError creates a new FieldNotFoundError with proper constraint,\n521\t\t\tValue:          value,\n522\t\t\tLine:           line,\n523\t\t\tErrorCode:      errorCode,\n524\t\t}\n525\t}\n526\t\n527\t// NewSyntaxError creates a new SyntaxError with proper found,\n536\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n537\t\t}\n538\t}\n539\t\n540\t// NewStructureError creates a new StructureError with proper initialization.\n541\tfunc NewStructureError(filePath string, message,\n545\t\t\tLine:         line,\n546\t\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n547\t\t\tLocation:     location,\n548\t\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n549\t\t}\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\t// NewDuplicateKeyError creates a new DuplicateKeyError with proper initialization.\n553\t//\n554\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n555\t// DuplicateKeyError instances with the given parameters.\n556\t//\n557\t// Parameters:\n558\t//   - filePath: Path to the file with duplicate keys\n559\t//   - key: The duplicate key name\n560\t//   - location: Nested path to the duplicate key (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n561\t//   - line1: Line number of first occurrence (use 0 if unknown)\n562\t//   - line2: Line number of duplicate occurrence (use 0 if unknown)\n563\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n564\t//\n565\t// Returns a properly initialized DuplicateKeyError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n566\tfunc NewDuplicateKeyError(filePath string, key string, location string, line1 int, line2 int, code ErrorCode) *DuplicateKeyError {\n567\t\t// Use provided code or default to duplicate key error\n568\t\terrorCode := code\n569\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n570\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n571\t\t}\n572\t\n573\t\treturn &DuplicateKeyError{\n574\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n575\t\t\tKey:       key,\n576\t\t\tLocation:  location,\n577\t\t\tLine1:     line1,\n578\t\t\tLine2:     line2,\n579\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n580\t\t}\n581\t}\n582\t\n583\t// NewSchemaLoadError creates a new SchemaLoadError with proper initialization.\n584\t//\n585\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n586\t// SchemaLoadError instances with the given parameters.\n587\t//\n588\t// Parameters:\n589\t//   - filePath: Path to the schema file\n590\t//   - message: Description of the load error\n591\t//   - err: Underlying error (optional, use nil if not applicable)\n592\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n593\t//\n594\t// Returns a properly initialized SchemaLoadError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n595\tfunc NewSchemaLoadError(filePath string, message string, err error, code ErrorCode) *SchemaLoadError {\n596\t\t// Use provided code or default to schema load failed error\n597\t\terrorCode := code\n598\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n599\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed\n600\t\t}\n601\t\n602\t\treturn &SchemaLoadError{\n603\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n604\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n605\t\t\tErr:       err,\n606\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n607\t\t}\n608\t}\n609\t\n610\t// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\n611\tfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n612\t\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n613\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n614\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n615\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n616\t\t\t\treturn true\n617\t\t\t}\n618\t\t}\n619\t\treturn false\n620\t}\n621\t\n622\t// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\n623\tfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n624\t\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n625\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n626\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n627\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n628\t\t\t\treturn true\n629\t\t\t}\n630\t\t}\n631\t\treturn false\n632\t}\n633\t\n634\t// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n635\t//\n636\t// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n637\t// information about the file operation that failed.\n638\ttype FileError struct {\n639\t\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n640\t\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n641\t\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n642\t\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n643\t\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n644\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n645\t}\n646\t\n647\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n648\tfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n649\t\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n650\t\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n651\t\t}\n652\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n653\t}\n654\t\n655\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n656\tfunc (fe *FileError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n657\t\treturn ErrorTypeFile\n658\t}\n659\t\n660\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n661\tfunc (fe *FileError) Context() string {\n662\t\top := fe.Operation\n663\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n664\t\t\top = fe.Op\n665\t\t}\n666\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n667\t}\n668\t\n669\t// Error implements the error interface.\n670\tfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n671\t\top := fe.Operation\n672\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n673\t\t\top = fe.Op\n674\t\t}\n675\t\n676\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n677\t\tif op != \"\" {\n678\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s\", op, fe.Path))\n679\t\t} else {\n680\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s\", fe.Path))\n681\t\t}\n682\t\n683\t\tif fe.Message != \"\" {\n684\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Message))\n685\t\t}\n686\t\n687\t\tif fe.Err != nil {\n688\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Err.Error()))\n689\t\t}\n690\t\n691\t\treturn sb.String()\n692\t}\n693\t\n694\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n695\tfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n696\t\treturn fe.Err\n697\t}\n698\t\n699\t// IsFileError checks if an error is a FileError.\n700\tfunc IsFileError(err error) bool {\n701\t\tvar fe *FileError\n702\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &fe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeFile))\n703\t}\n704\t\n705\t// SyntaxError represents YAML syntax errors during parsing.\n706\t//\n707\t// SyntaxError includes indentation errors, malformed YAML, and other\n708\t// syntax-related issues that prevent the YAML from being parsed.\n709\ttype SyntaxError struct {\n710\t\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with syntax error\n711\t\tLine      int       // Line number where syntax error occurred\n712\t\tColumn    int       // Column number where syntax error occurred\n713\t\tMessage   string    // Description of the syntax error\n714\t\tExpected  string    // What was expected (if known)\n715\t\tFound     string    // What was actually found (if known)\n716\t\tErr       error     // Underlying yaml parser error\n717\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n718\t}\n719\t\n720\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n721\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Code() ErrorCode {\n722\t\tif se.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n723\t\t\treturn se.ErrorCode\n724\t\t}\n725\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n726\t}\n727\t\n728\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n729\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n730\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n731\t}\n732\t\n733\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n734\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Context() string {\n735\t\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", se.Expected)\n736\t\tif se.Found != \"\" {\n737\t\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", found: %s\", se.Found)\n738\t\t}\n739\t\treturn ctx\n740\t}\n741\t\n742\t// Error implements the error interface.\n743\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Error() string {\n744\t\tif se.Line > 0 {\n745\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s at line %d, column %d: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Line, se.Column, se.Message)\n746\t\t}\n747\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Message)\n748\t}\n749\t\n750\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\n751\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Unwrap() error {\n752\t\treturn Code implements YAMLError interface.\n770\tfunc (ste *StructureError) Code() ErrorCode {\n771\t\tif ste.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n772\t\t\treturn ste.ErrorCode\n773\t\t}\n774\t\tif ste.DuplicateKey != \"\" *StructureError) Unwrap() error {\n804\t\treturn ste.Err\n805\t}\n806\t\n807\t// TypeMismatchError represents type conversion errors during parsing.\n808\t//\n809\t// TypeMismatchError occurs when tme.ActualType)\n848\t}\n849\t\n850\t// FieldNotFoundError represents an error when a required field is missing.\n851\t//\n852\t// FieldNotFoundError is used when accessing required fields that don't fnfe.FieldPath)\n885\t}\n886\t\n887\t// ConstraintError represents constraint validation failures.\n888\t//\n889\t// ConstraintError is used when a field value violates a defined constraint\n890\t// {\n362\t\t\tname      string\n363\t\t\terr       *EnhancedParseError\n364\t\t\tcheckFunc func(*EnhancedParseError) bool\n365\t\t\twant      bool\n366\t\t}{\n367\t\t\t{\"syntax error is syntax\", NewSyntaxParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, 1, \"\", \"\"),\n368\t\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsSyntaxError, true},\n369\t\t\t{\"syntax error is not structure\", NewSyntaxParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, 1, \"\", \"\"),\n370\t\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsStructureError, false},\n371\t\t\t{\"structure error is structure\", NewStructureParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\"),\n372\t\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsStructureError, true},\n373\t\t\t{\"structure error is not syntax\", NewStructureParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\"),\n374\t\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsSyntaxError, false},\n375\t\t\t{\"type mismatch is type mismatch\", NewTypeMismatchParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\", \"\", \"\"),\n376\t\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsTypeMismatchError, true},\n377\t\t\t{\"IO error is IO\", NewIOParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 0, nil),\n378\t\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsIOError, true},\n381\t\t\t{\"schema error is schema\", NewSchemaParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\"),\n382\t\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsSchemaError, true},\n383\t\t\t{\"empty error is empty\", NewEmptyParseError(\"test.yaml\"),\n384\t\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsEmpty, true},\n385\t\t}\n386\t\n387\t\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n388\t\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n389\t\t\t\tif got := tt.checkFunc(tt.err); got != tt.want {\n390\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"check function returned %v, want %v\", got, tt.want)\n391\t\t\t\t}\n392\t\t\t})\n393\t\t}\n394\t}\n395\t\n396\t// ============================================================================\n500\t// EnhancedParseError String Method Tests\n501\t// ============================================================================\n502\t\n503\tfunc TestEnhancedParseErrorString(t *testing.T) {\n504\t\tt.Run(\"error with snippet\", func(t *testing.T) {\n505\t\t\terr := NewSyntaxParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"bad syntax\", 5, 10, \":\", \"}\")\n506\t\t\terr.LocationInfo.Snippet = \"  port: 8080\"\n507\t\t\terr.LocationInfo.Column = 9\n508\t\n509\t\t\tresult := err.String()\n510\t\n511\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"syntax error\") {\n512\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected string to contain 'syntax error'\")\n513\t\t\t}\n514\t\n515\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"  port: 8080\") {\n516\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected string to contain snippet\")\n517\t\t\t}\n518\t\n519\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"^--- here\") {\n520\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected string to contain column indicator\")\n521\t\t\t}\n522\t\t})\n523\t\n524\t\tt.Run(\"error with surrounding lines\", func(t *testing.T) {\n525\t\t\terr := NewStructureParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"dup key\", 5, \"port\", \"server\")\n526\t\t\terr.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines = true\",\n532\t\t\t}\n533\t\t\terr.LocationInfo.SnippetLineIndex = 3\n534\t\n535\t\t\tresult := err.String()\n536\t\n537\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"structure error\") {\n538\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected string to contain 'structure error'\")\n539\t\t\t}\n540\t\n541\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \">   port: 8081\") {\n542\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected string to highlight error line with '>'\")\n543\t\t\t}\n544\t\n545\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"server:\") {\n546\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected string to contain surrounding context\")\n547\t\t\t}\n548\t\t})\n549\t\n550\t\tt.Run(\"error without extra context\", func(t *testing.T) {\n551\t\t\terr := NewEmptyParseError(\"empty.yaml\")\n552\t\n553\t\t\tresult := yamlutil\n6\t\n7\timport (\n8\t\t\"fmt\"\n9\t\t\"strings\"\n10\t)\n11\t\n12\t// ParseErrorKind represents the category of parse error.\n13\t// This serves as an enum-style discriminator for different error types.\n14\ttype ParseErrorKind string\n15\t\n16\tconst (\n17\t\t// ParseErrorKindSyntax indicates YAML syntax errors (indentation, malformed YAML, etc.)\n18\t\tParseErrorKindSyntax ParseErrorKind = \"syntax\"\n19\t\n20\t\t// ParseErrorKindStructure indicates YAML structure errors (duplicate keys, invalid nesting, etc.)\n21\t\tParseErrorKindStructure ParseErrorKind = \"structure\"\n22\t\n23\t\t// ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch indicates type conversion errors (string to int, etc.)\n24\t\tParseErrorKindTypeMismatch ParseErrorKind = \"type mismatch\"\n25\t\n26\t\t// ParseErrorKindIO indicates file I/O errors (not found, permission denied, etc.)\n27\t\tParseErrorKindIO ParseErrorKind = \"io\"\n28\t\n29\t\t// ParseErrorKindValidation indicates semantic validation errors (required fields, constraints, etc.)\n30\t\tParseErrorKindValidation ParseErrorKind = \"validation\"\n31\t\n32\t\t// ParseErrorKindSchema indicates schema validation errors\n33\t\tParseErrorKindSchema ParseErrorKind = \"schema\"\n34\t\n35\t\t// ParseErrorKindEmpty indicates empty file or content\n36\t\tParseErrorKindEmpty ParseErrorKind = \"empty\"\n37\t\n38\t\t// ParseErrorKindUnknown indicates an unknown error type\n39\t\tParseErrorKindUnknown ParseErrorKind = \"unknown\"\n40\t)\n41\t\n42\t// ParseErrorDetail contains specific details for different error kinds.\n43\t// Only relevant fields are populated based on the ErrorKind.\n44\ttype ParseErrorDetail struct {\n45\t\t// Syntax-specific fields\n46\t\tExpected string // What was expected (for syntax errors)\n47\t\tFound    string // What was actually found\n48\t\n49\t\t// Structure-specific fields\n50\t\tDuplicateKey string // Name of duplicate key (for structure errors)\n51\t\tLocation     string // Nested path to error location\n52\t\n53\t\t// Type mismatch-specific fields\n54\t\tFieldPath    string // Dot-notation path to field (for type errors)\n55\t\tExpectedType string // Expected type\n56\t\tActualType   string // Actual type found\n57\t\tValue        string // Actual value that caused error\n58\t\n59\t\t// Validation-specific fields\n60\t\tConstraintType string // Type of constraint (range, length, pattern, etc.)\n61\t\tConstraint     string // Description of constraint\n62\t\n63\t\t// Schema-specific fields\n64\t\tSchemaPath string // Path to schema file\n65\t\tSchemaName string // Name of schema\n66\t}\n67\t\n68\t// ErrorContext provides rich context about the error location.\n69\ttype ErrorContext struct {\n70\t\t// Line is the line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n71\t\tLine int\n72\t\n73\t\t// Column is the column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n74\t\tColumn int\n75\t\n76\t\t// Snippet is the line of source code where error occurred\n77\t\tSnippet string\n78\t\n79\t\t// SurroundingLines provides context around the error line\n80\t\tSurroundingLines []string\n81\t\n82\t\t// SnippetLineIndex is the index of the error line within SurroundingLines\n83\t\tSnippetLineIndex int\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// EnhancedParseError provides comprehensive error information for parsing failures.\n87\t//\n88\t// This enhanced error type includes:\n89\t// - Error kind categorization (syntax, structure, type mismatch, etc.)\n90\t// - Detailed error-specific information\n91\t// - Rich context including source snippets\n92\t// - Integration with Result<T, ParseError> pattern\n93\ttype EnhancedParseError struct {\n94\t\t// Kind categorizes the error type\n95\t\tKind ParseErrorKind\n96\t\n97\t\t// FilePath is the path to the file being parsed\n98\t\tFilePath string\n99\t\n100\t\t// Message is a human-readable error message\n101\t\tMessage string\n102\t\n103\t\t// Context provides location and source context information\n104\t\tLocationInfo ErrorContext\n105\t\n106\t\t// Detail contains error-specific details\n107\t\tDetail ParseErrorDetail\n108\t\n109\t\t// UnderlyingErr is the wrapped error for compatibility\n110\t\tUnderlyingErr error\n111\t}\n112\t\n113\t// Error implements the error interface.\n114\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Error() string {\n115\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n116\t\n117\t\t// Build error header\n118\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n119\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s at line %d\", e.Kind, e.FilePath, e.LocationInfo.Line))\n120\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n121\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n122\t\t\t}\n123\t\t} else {\n124\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s\", e.Kind, e.FilePath))\n125\t\t}\n126\t\n127\t\t// Add kind-specific details\n128\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n129\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n130\t\t\t// For type mismatch, if we have field info, format it specially\n131\t\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n132\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", field %s: expected %s, got: %s\",\n133\t\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.ExpectedType, e.Detail.ActualType))\n134\t\t\t\t// Add parenthetical with full details (note: uses \"got:\" for consistency)\n135\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, expected: %s, got: %s)\",\n136\t\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.ExpectedType, e.Detail.ActualType))\n137\t\t\t} else if e.Message != \"\" {\n138\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n139\t\t\t}\n140\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n141\t\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n142\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n143\t\t\t}\n144\t\t\tif e.Detail.Expected != \"\" || e.Detail.Found != \"\" {\n145\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %s, found: %s)\", e.Detail.Expected, e.Detail.Found))\n146\t\t\t}\n147\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n148\t\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n149\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n150\t\t\t}\n151\t\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n152\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (duplicate key: %s\", e.Detail.DuplicateKey))\n153\t\t\t\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n154\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n155\t\t\t\t}\n156\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n157\t\t\t}\n158\t\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n159\t\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n160\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n161\t\t\t}\n162\t\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" && e.Detail.Constraint != \"\" {\n163\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, constraint: %s)\",\n164\t\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.Constraint))\n165\t\t\t}\n166\t\tdefault:\n167\t\t\t// For other error types, just add the message\n168\t\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n169\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n170\t\t\t}\n171\t\t}\n172\t\n173\t\treturn sb.String()\n174\t}\n175\t\n176\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\n177\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Unwrap() error {\n178\t\treturn e.UnderlyingErr\n179\t}\n180\t\n181\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic handling.\n182\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n183\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n184\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n185\t\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n186\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n187\t\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n188\t\t\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n189\t\t\t}\n190\t\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n191\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n192\t\t\treturn ErrCodeTypeMismatch\n193\t\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n194\t\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n195\t\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n196\t\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n197\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n198\t\t\treturn ErrCodeSchemaValidation\n199\t\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n200\t\t\treturn ErrCodeFileEmpty\n201\t\tdefault:\n202\t\t\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n203\t\t}\n204\t}\n205\t\n206\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n207\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n208\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n209\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n210\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n211\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n212\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n213\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n214\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeTypeMismatch\n215\t\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n216\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeIO\n217\t\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n218\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n219\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n220\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeSchema\n221\t\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n222\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeEmpty\n223\t\tdefault:\n224\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeUnknown\n225\t\t}\n226\t}\n227\t\n228\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n229\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Context() string {\n230\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n231\t\n232\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n233\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"line: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Line))\n234\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n235\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n236\t\t\t}\n237\t\t}\n238\t\n239\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n240\t\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n241\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n242\t\t\t}\n243\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s\", e.Detail.FieldPath))\n244\t\t}\n245\t\n246\t\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n247\t\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n248\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n249\t\t\t}\n250\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n251\t\t}\n252\t\n253\t\treturn sb.String()\n254\t}\n255\t\n256\t// String returns a detailed multi-line error representation with source snippet.\n257\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) String() string {\n258\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n259\t\n260\t\t// Error header\n261\t\tsb.WriteString(e.Error())\n262\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n263\t\n264\t\t// Show snippet with caret pointer if we have it\n265\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n266\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n267\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")  // Add 2-space prefix for consistency\n268\t\t\tsb.WriteString(e.LocationInfo.Snippet)\n269\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n270\t\n271\t\t\t// Add column indicator if available\n272\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 && e.LocationInfo.Column <= len(e.LocationInfo.Snippet) {\n273\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")  // Add 2-space prefix for consistency\n274\t\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < e.LocationInfo.Column-1; i++ {\n275\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" \")\n276\t\t\t\t}\n277\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"^--- here\\n\")\n278\t\t\t}\n279\t\t}\n280\t\n281\t\t// Add surrounding context if available\n282\t\tif len(e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines) > 0 {\n283\t\t\t// Add blank line separator if we showed the snippet\n284\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n285\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n286\t\t\t}\n287\t\t\tfor i, line := range e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines {\n288\t\t\t\tif i == e.LocationInfo.SnippetLineIndex {\n289\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"> \")\n290\t\t\t\t} else {\n291\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n292\t\t\t\t}\n293\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(line)\n294\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n295\t\t\t}\n296\t\t}\n297\t\n298\t\treturn sb.String()\n299\t}\n300\t\n301\t// IsSyntaxError checks if this is a syntax error.\n302\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSyntaxError() bool {\n303\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSyntax\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t// IsStructureError checks if this is a structure error.\n307\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsStructureError() bool {\n308\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindStructure\n309\t}\n310\t\n311\t// IsTypeMismatchError checks if this is a type mismatch error.\n312\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsTypeMismatchError() bool {\n313\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch\n314\t}\n315\t\n316\t// IsIOError checks if this is an I/O error.\n317\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsIOError() bool {\n318\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindIO\n319\t}\n320\t\n321\t// IsValidationError checks if this is a validation error.\n322\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsValidationError() bool {\n323\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindValidation\n324\t}\n325\t\n326\t// IsSchemaError checks if this is a schema error.\n327\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSchemaError() bool {\n328\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSchema\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// IsEmpty checks if this is an empty file error.\n332\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsEmpty() bool {\n333\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindEmpty\n334\t}\n335\t\n336\t// ============================================================================\n337\t// Error Construction Helpers\n338\t// ============================================================================\n339\t\n340\t// NewSyntaxParseError creates a new syntax error.\n341\tfunc NewSyntaxParseError(filePath, message string,
